findings.problem: The chapter clearly lands Leela's ethical failure, but June's direct technical loss stays partly inferential. After repeated requests for node C and the hash script, the text mostly gives us texts and scheduler status rather than one concrete on-page failure caused by Leela's refusal, so the promised consequence reads more as Leela's guilt than as fully dramatized material harm to June.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the late submission lead-up around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:323 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:365 so June's damage becomes materially legible before the upload at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:391. Put one specific loss on the page: a failed check that the withheld script would have narrowed, a missed meeting consequence, or a run that dies because Leela keeps the block live.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can point to at least one explicit technical or professional setback for June in the revised passage anchored near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:323 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:365 and explain how Leela's choice caused or materially worsened it without having to infer the link. Fail if June's cost is still conveyed only through angry texts, scheduler screenshots, or accusation.

findings.problem: Mohit's morning phone call largely restates an argument the office scene has already won: scoped claim over broader truth, submission over scruple, June as collateral. Because the later scene adds little new leverage until the very end, the final act loses some escalation and starts to feel rhetorically repetitive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:327 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:359 so the call either introduces a genuinely new pressure vector or gets compressed. If you keep the scene, make Mohit force a fresh choice that was not already established in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:163, rather than rephrasing the same scope-versus-integrity logic.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:327, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:359 changes Leela's options or stakes in a way not already established at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:163. Fail if the call still mainly repeats Mohit's earlier language about framing, scope, and June being secondary.

findings.problem: The prose slips into clipped narration fragments that violate the constitution's anti-elliptical rule and read like shorthand rather than earned compression. "Coherent contribution.", "Negative again.", "Not loud. Almost tired.", and "A paper ID. A receipt." all depend on omitted function words or implied subjects to create force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:393 into grammatically complete sentences that keep the same pressure without subject-dropping or article-stripping. Preserve the pace, but let the rhythm come from syntax and image rather than clipped fragments.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if those four cited passages read as complete sentences in narration while preserving the scene pressure. Fail if standalone narration fragments equivalent to "Coherent contribution.", "Negative again.", "Not loud. Almost tired.", or "A paper ID. A receipt." remain.

findings.problem: The exact pressure template "hard enough to make ..." appears three times in different contexts. By the third use, the chapter is reusing the same sentence engine for June's cup, the unstable seed, and the buzzing phone, which flattens otherwise sharp prose into a detectable tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:109, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:225 so those beats no longer rely on the same "hard enough to make ..." construction. Keep the physical force in each moment, but give each image its own syntax.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter no longer contains the exact "hard enough to make ..." construction in all three cited locations and each beat reads with a distinct sentence shape. Fail if all three passages still use the same template.

summary: Chapter 4 keeps past-tense close third, lands the bodily deadline pressure, and maintains strong voice separation for Leela, June, and Mohit; it also avoids sanitizing Leela's compromise. It still fails review because June's direct technical loss is not rendered concretely enough on the page, the late Mohit call repeats an already-established argument, and the prose shows both clipped-fragment drift and a repeated sentence template.
